title: Originating AFT via File Transmission (BC)
body_text:
Originating AFT via File Transmission (BC)
1	AFT File Transmission Overview
1.1	Introduction to AFT File Transmission Service
Originators that create AFT transactions on software not supported by Central 1 must submit AFT files to Central 1 via file transmission. The Originator sends the AFT file directly to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. The file format must adhere to Central 1âs AFT File format as published in Central 1âs AFT File Specifications, Form 2726. These specifications are based on CPA Standard 005, Standards for the Exchange of Financial Data on AFT Files.
These Originators use in-house or third party software to generate their AFT transactions. The database containing the AFT transactions is maintained and held by the Originator or by a party contracted to do so; it is not held at Central 1. As a result, Central 1 is unable to support these software products. Contact your Technology Department or software provider for assistance with software-related problems.
The financial institution is responsible for setting up business member Originators, for maintaining business membersâ accounts at the financial institution, for monitoring AFT reports for rejects and recalls, and for posting rejects and recalls to the business membersâ financial institution accounts.

1.2	Contacts and Websites

Contact Information
Contact Payments Canada to access the Financial Institutions Branch Directory, to get a list of CPA AFT transaction codes, or to access other information relevant to the Canadian Payments Association.

Website:
www.payments.ca

the File and Report Exchange

retrieval of MERG files or missing MERG files
transaction or file limit increases*
modifications or cancellation of AFT Service.*

2. To request a copy of the Monthly Report â Regulations Implementing the United Nations Resolutions on the Suppression of Terrorism, go to:
